commit.message:
Fix Sentry false positives on successful signup

The sendDiagnosticReport was being called unconditionally after every
signup, causing successful signups to appear as errors in Sentry.

Moved sendDiagnosticReport inside the error handling block so it only
sends to Sentry when profile creation actually fails.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: use Redfin MLS address as primary, ArcGIS situs as fallback only

The build pipeline was unconditionally overriding the Redfin address with
the ArcGIS parcel situs address. This caused wrong addresses on corner lots
where the assessor's situs uses the commercial frontage (e.g. 1301 Abbot
Kinney Blvd) while the MLS listing uses the residential side (558 San Juan
Ave). Now situs is only used when Redfin address is missing.

commit.message:
refactor: APIs-first architecture — data brief pre-injects into AI prompt

Major pipeline restructuring:

1. PHASE 0 (new): All API fetches move BEFORE the AI phase. Live data from
   CoinGecko, Deribit, Dune, DefiLlama, Alternative.me, Etherscan now
   collected first and formatted into a 15-line "data brief."

2. DATA BRIEF: Pre-loaded numbers injected directly into the AI system prompt.
   The AI sees hard data upfront and acts as analyst, not data collector.

3. SEARCH REDUCTION: 21 searches cut to 15. Six searches eliminated where
   APIs already cover the params (derivatives, staking APY, stablecoins,
   Fear & Greed, ETH/BTC, L2 TVL). Remaining searches focus on narrative,
   qualitative signals, and context that APIs cannot provide.

4. ROLE SHIFT: System prompt rewritten from "search and report" to "here is
   the data, analyze it." AI focuses on divergence detection, synthesis,
   and qualitative analysis.

5. MVRV CACHE: Weekly Dune query for MVRV proxy data, cached in
   .mvrv_cache.json to avoid burning credits daily.

6. LEARNINGS: extract_learnings() now tracks API-backed param count per run.

Expected: ~30% token savings per run, faster execution, higher analysis quality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: eth-alpha
repository.description: eth alpha tracker

commit.message:
fix: defer fetchData until auth is ready (root cause of empty dashboard)

fetchData and realtime subscriptions were firing on component mount,
before AuthProvider had resolved the session. Supabase queries hit RLS
with no auth token, returned empty results, and the .catch() fallbacks
produced an empty dashboard.

Since fetchData's useEffect was defined before the authLoading early
return, it ran immediately regardless of auth state. The empty dependency
array meant it never re-fired when auth resolved.

Fix: Both useEffects (fetchData + realtime channel) now depend on
authLoading and only execute when !authLoading. This ensures the
Supabase client has a valid session before any queries run.

Deployed to https://apex-crm-chi.vercel.app

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
PFC circuit: recurrent GRU reading all layers for task representation

Replace single-layer sensory snapshot with proper PFC:
- Reads activation summary from ALL 4 patch layers (early=concrete, late=abstract)
- Own GRU (32-dim) maintains stable task representation across steps
- Separate from critic GRU: critic predicts reward, PFC represents task context
- Per-patch context gates from PFC state

Redesign corner mount as 4-sided shallow box

Complete redesign from scratch:
- 4-sided box with 7mm thick floor and 4mm walls
- Internal cavity: 30mm x 30mm x laptop thickness
- Forms complete perimeter around laptop corner
- Top retention tabs on two adjacent walls (front and left)
- Slot mount base on OUTSIDE of left wall (not inside pocket)
- Base positioned centered on exterior wall face, vertically centered

Laptop corner sits in box, retention tabs prevent lift.
Mount attaches to shelf via external base - no interference with laptop.

feat: artistic black hole shader -- no raytracing, designed visual elements

Replaces geodesic raytracer with hand-designed shader. Each visual element
(disk, photon ring, lensing, void, haze, stars) is painted directly with
smooth math. The disk band wraps around the void by expanding its angular
width near the event horizon, simulating gravitational lensing without
per-pixel ray integration. Much faster, no step artifacts, fully controllable.

commit.message:
Add autoCloseTags and HTML completion to lang-html (#22)

Ports autoCloseTags, htmlCompletionSource, htmlCompletionSourceWith, and
HtmlSchema with full tag/attribute data from @codemirror/lang-html:

- HtmlCloseTags.kt: autoCloseTags extension that auto-inserts closing tags
  when the user types `>` or `/` inside an open tag
- HtmlComplete.kt: htmlCompletionSource and htmlCompletionSourceWith for
  context-aware HTML tag and attribute completion; HtmlSchema with the full
  DefaultTags and DefaultGlobalAttrs data sets (including ARIA attributes
  and event handlers); HtmlCompletionConfig for schema extension
- Html.kt: re-exports autoCloseTags, htmlCompletionSource,
  htmlCompletionSourceWith, and HtmlSchema from the module's public API
- lang-html.api updated to reflect new public symbols

commit.message:
Fix billback loading: add server-side cache for UBI unassigned

The /api/billback/ubi/unassigned endpoint was re-scanning all of
DynamoDB (assignments + archived) and 90 days of S3 on every page
request, making pagination useless. With growing DDB tables this
caused infinite spinner.

Fix: compute unassigned bills once, cache in memory for 5 min.
Subsequent page requests served instantly from cache. Cache is
invalidated on assign/unassign/reassign/archive mutations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix useRouter() outside Vue setup context, clean up API client and map composables

- Replace useRouter()/useRoute() with direct router import in apiClient.ts
  and jwt.ts — composables only work inside setup(), making auth redirects
  silently fail in service files
- Remove duplicate passAuthCheckOrExit call after successful fetch
- Fix GET requests incorrectly sending a body (only include body when defined)
- Simplify verifyAuth logic (single clear flow instead of try/catch/rethrow)
- Use spread operator instead of Object.assign for headers
- Type fetchOptions as RequestInit
- Fix uninitialized currentMapset in useMapLayers.ts (null safety)
- Fix self-assignment bug in useLayerVisibility.ts (currentlyHiddenLayers)
- Merge redundant condition branches (isDefaultMapset || mapsetHasPreferred)
- Use .some() instead of .filter().length for existence check
- Remove excessive blank lines and redundant comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix shadow/tray persisting during Tetris fall: remove overlays at top of onPointerUp

Remove ghost, shadow and pileShadow DOM elements at the very start of
onPointerUp, before any board logic runs. Previously they were removed
inside cleanupDrag() which runs after place() → clearFullWithExplosion()
→ drawBoard() — too late on some code paths.

feat(v12): Phase 3 — ability effects system

Server: ability_engine.go with 6 auto-trigger conditions (always_active,
hp_below_30pct, enemy_in_range, chase_or_flee, on_contact, orb_dense_area),
cooldown management, and gameplay effects (damage, invincibility, speed boost,
mass drain, gravity pull). Event pipeline: ability_triggered through protocol,
events, arena, room, serializer (ab/tx/ty/abl fields in state packets).

Client: AbilityEffects.tsx with InstancedMesh-pooled rendering for 6 effects —
Venom Aura (green particle ring), Shield Burst (blue hemisphere expand),
Lightning Strike (yellow cylinder bolts + flash), Speed Dash (cyan trails),
Mass Drain (purple beam + particle arc), Gravity Well (torus vortex + spiral).

commit.message:
Add auto-expand search: finds pages in collapsed branches

Search now walks the entire tree (including collapsed subtrees via
_allChildren) to find matches. When a match is found inside a
collapsed branch, all ancestor nodes are automatically expanded.
Zooms to the first match at a readable scale.

Pre-search collapse state is saved and restored when the search
box is cleared, so the tree returns to its original state.
Uses 300ms debounce to avoid expensive tree updates on every
keystroke.

commit.message:
feat: add multi-tile raster mosaic support for mixed-CRS inputs

Enables processing raster datasets distributed as multiple UTM-zone tiles
(e.g., RAP 10m Sentinel-2 covering Wyoming in zones 12 and 13) into a
single WGS84 COG without manual preprocessing.

- `cog.py`: add `create_mosaic_cog()` that groups tiles by CRS, warps
  each group to target CRS via separate VRTs, merges, and writes COG;
  update `RasterProcessor.__init__` to accept `Union[str, List[str]]`
- `cli.py`: `--input` and `--source-url` now accept multiple values;
  add `--target-crs`, `--target-extent`, `--target-resolution`, `--band`
- `workflows.py`: extend `generate_raster_workflow()` to detect when a
  preprocess-cog step is needed (multiple tiles, target_extent, or band);
  add `_generate_cog_preprocess_job()` (8 CPU, 32Gi, 200Gi, no-GPU) that
  runs `cng-datasets raster` to mosaic tiles before the hex tiling step
- `test_k8s_workflows.py`: add 16 unit tests covering the new raster
  workflow paths (preprocess triggers, job content, resource limits,
  hex job routing, orchestrator configmap, GPU avoidance)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): address flaky test infrastructure issues (#7706)

* fix(ci): address flaky test infrastructure issues

- Add readiness wait after `docker compose up -d testagent` so tests
  don't start sending multipart data before busboy is ready
- Copy servicebus emulator config file and restart emulator in the
  azure-service-bus CI job (queue.1/topic.1 were missing because
  GitHub Actions services don't support volume mounts; the
  azure-functions job already does this with docker cp + restart)
- Wait for emulator to be ready after restart before running tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): add SQL Edge health check and emulator ready-wait for azure-service-bus

SQL Server Edge takes 45-90s to start; without a health check the Service Bus
emulator connects before SQL is ready. Add a health check using bash TCP
built-in (nc is not installed in the azure-sql-edge image) so GitHub Actions
gates job steps until SQL is healthy. After docker restart, wait for port 5672
to be ready before running tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): remove unnecessary emulator config copy from azure-service-bus job

The azure-service-bus plugin tests use the built-in OOB emulator config
(queue.1, topic.1), so copying a custom config file and restarting the
emulator is not needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add batch CRUD operations to FirestoreCollectionRepository (#12)

* feat: Add batch CRUD operations to FirestoreCollectionRepository (#11)

Add batchAdd, batchSet, batchPatch, and batchDelete methods that use
Firestore WriteBatch to perform multiple operations atomically. This
bypasses the Command isRunning guard that prevents concurrent calls
to the single-item CRUD commands.

All methods automatically chunk into 500-op batches to stay within
Firestore limits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review: Extract _runBatched helper, add batchUpdate, chunking tests

- DRY: consolidate chunking loop into _runBatched<E> generic helper
- Add batchUpdate for full-document replacement (matches update command)
- Make batchLimit a public static const for test access
- Add chunking boundary tests (batchLimit + N items) for batchDelete/batchAdd
- Improve doc comments: mention isRunning bypass, link to batchLimit, add
  code examples with proper dartdoc formatting
- Update barrel file docs to mention batch operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: Make batch operations Commands instead of plain Futures

Batch operations (batchAdd, batchSet, batchPatch, batchUpdate,
batchDelete) are now Command instances, consistent with single-item
CRUD. This enables watching isRunning, listening to errors, and
the full Command lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(panorama): structural drilldown rewrite — real DB tech_ids, payload mapper, deterministic editorial

CAUSA RAIZ DO 404
- typescript, python, rust não existem na sofia.tech_registry
- openai→openai-sdk, torch→pytorch, anthropic→anthropic-sdk (mismatch corrigido)
- todos os tech_ids dos chips verificados contra /api/tech/radar/top

SHAPE MISMATCH CORRIGIDO
- API retorna { tech, snapshots, papers, geo, meta }
- componente agora usa mapApiResponse() que converte payload rico → shape interno correto
- momentum_score, stars_growth_30d, downloads_growth_30d, etc. mapeados corretamente

HARDCODE REMOVIDO COMO FONTE PRIMÁRIA
- TECH_EDITORIAL_PT e TECH_EDITORIAL_EN eliminados do TechPulseTabPanel
- drilldown agora deriva conteúdo DETERMINISTICAMENTE dos dados da API

LÓGICA DETERMINÍSTICA (buildTechEditorial)
- role: derivado de ecosystem + source do DB
- themes: construídos dos growth ratios não-nulos (downloads, stars, pulls, papers, contributors)
- interpretation: regras explícitas baseadas em avgGrowth:
  > 1.30 → "crescimento acelerado"
  > 1.08 → "crescimento consistente"
  >= 0.95 → "volume estável"
  < 0.95 → "perdeu intensidade"
  null → "janela histórica em formação"
- signals_used: audit trail dos sinais usados

TOPICS/ENTITIES
- kind='topic' ou 'entity' → buildTopicAnalysis() usando contexto do tab (platform, growth, volume)
- sem chamada de API para topics

FALLBACK TÉCNICO EXPLÍCITO
- quando API falha para kind='tech': mostra painel FALLBACK com label vermelho
- não mascara erro, documenta o problema

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extract MicBar, apply to FreeShadow, clean state on re-record

- Extract MicBar into shared component used by both LessonView and
  FreeShadow (replaces old inline MicSelector in both)
- FreeShadow now has floating mic bar at bottom with auto-recovery,
  liveness detection, and re-enable support
